L4.1.summary: Introduce a workflow_dispatch evidence path with minimal coupling to runtime governance.
notes: This slice should only depend on workflow-change execution safety, not on pre-existing release sidecar evidence.
Keep the future active workflow focused on evidence export, not on full repository governance.

L4.2.summary: Emit a structured release sidecar that future promotion logic can ingest.
notes: Keep sidecar fields limited to release and evidence facts.
Do not turn product-domain data into release-governance payload.

L4.3.summary: Unify script-driven and workflow-driven release policy into one authoritative path.
notes: Pick one authority for release publication and make the other path subordinate or remove it.
Do not let loop promotion reason over two competing release channels.

L5.1.summary: Implement the active DataPulse adapter after blueprint-first contracts are accepted.
notes: Keep adapter logic slice-profile-driven.
Keep service-governance evidence behind adapter lookups.

L7.1.summary: Upgrade the deploy surface into a stateful mission deck while keeping the shell Reader-backed.
notes: Keep the browser shell aligned with existing Reader contracts.
Treat the mission deck as an operating surface, not as a GUI-only domain model.

L7.2.summary: Add server-derived mission recommendations, duplicate pressure hints, and route guidance to the deck.
notes: Prefer Reader-derived state over client-only heuristics where recommendation quality matters.
Conflict hints should help operators avoid silent mission duplication.

L7.3.summary: Add a command palette and keyboard control layer to the browser shell.
notes: The palette should expose existing mission/story actions instead of inventing new nouns.
Shortcut coverage belongs to the shell, not to one panel.

L7.4.summary: Add action history, undo surfaces, and optimistic board mutation feedback.
notes: Undo should use real reversible backend routes where possible.
Loading states must keep the shell readable during board refresh.

L7.5.summary: Verify the uplift through browser-level smoke before any manual loop ignition.
notes: Keep browser smoke manual-capable so operators can validate the shell before local Codex ignition.
Do not silently convert browser smoke into a mandatory lightweight gate for every fast smoke path.

L8.1.summary: Translate the external commercial-intelligence note into a repo-specific governance blueprint and follow-up slice map.
notes: Keep the document repo-scoped: map the external note onto DataPulse objects instead of restating generic market-intelligence theory.
Use the document to justify future blueprint slices, not to bypass the blueprint.

L8.2.summary: Define a unified source-governance contract for public-source intelligence intake, covering source class, collection mode, authority, sensitivity, and compliance hints.
notes: Treat public-web, API, search-gateway, and manual fact intake as first-class collection modes.
Do not let the contract imply that DataPulse auto-decides legal compliance; keep it to governance hints and operator-visible boundaries.

L8.3.summary: Project analyst demand-intent fields into WatchMission and mission-facing surfaces without forking lifecycle semantics.
notes: Map KIT/KIQ-like ideas into repo language such as demand intent, scope, freshness, and coverage targets.
Keep WatchMission as the durable mission-intent object across CLI, MCP, and console.

L8.4.summary: Carry evidence-grade, provenance, and delivery-risk governance through triage, story, and alert outputs.
candidate_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py tests/test_alerts.py -q
notes: Evidence-grade semantics must preserve provenance back to items, review state, and sources.
Delivery risk belongs in alert and route observations, not as a replacement for underlying evidence truth.

L8.5.summary: Expose intelligence governance scorecard signals in reader and console ops surfaces.
notes: Prefer scorecard signals that reflect intelligence usefulness, not only collector uptime.
Coverage, freshness, alert yield, triage throughput, and story conversion should stay derivable from existing lifecycle objects.

L9.1.summary: Distill the external content-acquisition and fact-screening note into a repo-scoped reinforcement blueprint and slice map.
notes: Keep the document repo-scoped: explain why native Chinese-source reinforcement and fact screening are higher delta than generic crawler replacement.
Use the document to justify follow-up slices, not to bypass the blueprint with a prose dump.

L9.2.summary: Define the native Chinese collector bridge contract, shortlist, fallback policy, and provenance rules.
notes: The contract should define sidecar invocation, session/env dependencies, normalized ParseResult mapping, and provenance markers before any native collector implementation lands.
Keep the shortlist focused on MediaCrawler, wechat_spider, weiboSpider, and GeneralNewsExtractor-class integrations.

L9.3.summary: Implement a MediaCrawler-class sidecar bridge for native Chinese platform collection without displacing existing fallbacks.
notes: Prefer a sidecar boundary over vendoring third-party collector repos into the tree.
Bridge provenance must remain visible so operators know when a native path, Jina path, or browser fallback was used.

L9.4.summary: Add a first native WeChat article collector path while preserving the current Jina/browser safety net.
notes: WeChat should prefer the native path only when its prerequisites are satisfied and should otherwise fall back cleanly.
Keep setup hints and operator-visible failure modes explicit.

L9.5.summary: Add a first native Weibo collector path to the router as a first-class source type.
notes: Treat Weibo as a direct router gap, not as a side effect of generic extraction.
Keep router order explicit so the new collector does not degrade existing platform handling.

L9.6.summary: Add a Chinese news body extraction backend to the generic collector chain.
notes: This slice should improve Chinese正文 quality without replacing the current generic collector contract.
Expose backend provenance so operators can distinguish targeted正文 extraction from the existing fallback chain.

L9.7.summary: Project grounded claim and evidence extraction into triage and story surfaces.
notes: Grounded extraction must preserve source-linked claims and evidence spans rather than generating unanchored summaries.
Keep the outputs compatible with existing evidence governance instead of creating a parallel claim store first.

L9.8.summary: Add a factuality gate before digest, story export, and alert escalation.
notes: Place factuality scoring at the delivery trust boundary, not inside low-level collection.
The gate should expose operator-visible trust signals instead of silently rewriting evidence truth.

L9.9.summary: Add trend-intelligence feed inputs for watch seeding without replacing URL collectors.
notes: Trend inputs should seed missions and feeds, not pretend to be a replacement for item-level URL collection.
Keep trend signals distinguishable from directly collected evidence.

L9.10.summary: Define a manual emergency acquisition lane for EasySpider, Crawlab, and f2 class tools.
notes: Keep operator-driven tooling in an explicit manual lane instead of coupling it into the core collector contract.
Define handoff rules, artifacts, and provenance expectations for emergency or manual collection workflows.

L10.1.summary: Define optional grounding and factuality backend contracts, fallback semantics, and provenance rules.
notes: The contract should formalize backend invocation boundaries, operator-visible provenance, and deterministic fallback behavior before any adapter lands.
Do not reopen collector architecture; this phase is only about backend enrichment behind the new evidence surfaces.

L10.2.summary: Add a LangExtract-class grounding adapter behind the current triage and story grounding boundary.
notes: Preserve current `provided / heuristic / empty` grounding modes as the fallback contract.
Backend output must enrich the existing governance payload rather than inventing a new claim object model.

L10.3.summary: Add an OpenFactVerification-class backend option at the factuality gate boundary.
candidate_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_story.py tests/test_alerts.py tests/test_digest.py -q
notes: Keep the existing deterministic factuality gate as the stable fallback when the backend is unavailable or disabled.
Backend verdicts and signals should stay operator-visible and additive rather than silently replacing current reasoning.

L22.2.summary: Add grapheme-aware truncation utilities and use them in shared excerpt-sensitive surfaces before any browser-only measurement work.
notes: Keep the first ignition target deterministic and shared across CLI, Reader, Markdown export, and console surfaces.
Do not split emoji or grapheme clusters just to preserve previous character-count behavior.

L22.3.summary: Add cached canvas width fitting for dense console labels, chips, and rail summaries where CSS ellipsis is insufficient.
notes: Apply pixel-fit measurement only to dense populated surfaces with proven readability issues.
Keep canvas fitting optional and retain a deterministic truncation fallback when measurement is unavailable.

L23.2.summary: Add repo-owned runtime overflow evidence for residual console text surfaces after the landed grapheme-safe truncation and cached fit layers run.
notes: Prefer evidence that names residual hotspots and survivor counts over screenshot-only inspection.
Keep this slice local to console ergonomics; do not overload the AI-surface runtime-hit exporters with unrelated text-overflow semantics unless later reuse pressure proves it is the right home.

L24.2.summary: Freeze the URL-restorable workspace-state, section-summary, and operator-guidance contract before the next console-clarity implementation slice lands.
notes: Preserve the existing command chamber shell and current lifecycle nouns.
Treat this slice as a contract freeze for URL state, section summary semantics, and guidance ownership rather than as a fresh implementation burst.

L24.3.summary: Add URL-restorable workspace context and section-level success or blocker cards to the current console shell.
notes: Extend the current hash and story parameter model instead of replacing it with a second routing system.
Section summary cards should expose current objective plus success or blocker signals without introducing GUI-only domain logic.

L24.4.summary: Centralize operator guidance and action-explanation surfaces across mission, triage, story, and route lanes.
notes: Reuse existing retry guidance, duplicate explain, and deck suggestion semantics instead of re-deriving explanations from scratch.
Keep the text contract operator-facing, explicit, and shared across surfaces.

L24.5.summary: Extract console client or state helpers and harden restored-context browser smoke after the new interaction contract lands.
notes: Treat extraction as maintainability hardening, not as a justification for a new frontend stack.
Smoke should prove restored-context behavior, not only basic page load and route reachability.

L25.1.summary: Promote the workflow-first shell simplification judgment into repo truth and reopen the next console wave through a narrow new blueprint.
notes: Open a new wave because `L24` is already completed; do not overload the interaction-clarity wave with shell-shape simplification.
Use this slice to make `L25.2` the unambiguous next ignition target before any shell rewrite begins.

L25.2.summary: Freeze the workflow-first shell contract, first-rank versus demoted surface rules, stage-owned outputs, and feedback ownership.
notes: Freeze structure before implementation so the shell does not drift while simplifying same-rank surfaces.
Do not reopen frontend-stack migration or URL-state contract questions inside this slice unless the new workflow-first contract proves a direct conflict.

L25.3.summary: Reorganize the default shell around workflow-first stages and demote advanced surfaces that should not compete for first-rank attention.
notes: Preserve the command chamber identity and canonical lifecycle nouns while simplifying shell rank and sequence.
Prefer nested detail views, drawers, or stage-owned tabs over continuing to expose every capability at the same default rank.

L25.4.summary: Move no-result, warning, completion, and blocked-state semantics onto the stage or card that owns the work instead of relying on toasts alone.
notes: Keep toasts for temporary acknowledgements, but move persistent or corrective states onto the owned surface.
Empty-state, warning, and blocked-state copy should tell operators why the surface is empty and what to do next.

L25.5.summary: Harden browser smoke and acceptance around workflow order, owned outputs, and next-step discoverability after the shell is simplified.
notes: Smoke should prove stage order, stage-owned outputs, next-step clarity, and recovery from no-result or blocked states.
Treat comprehension regressions as first-class failures; route reachability alone is not sufficient for this slice.

L26.1.summary: Promote the remaining GUI follow-up gaps into repo truth through a new modularity-and-traceability blueprint wave.
notes: Open a new wave because `L25` is already completed; do not stretch the shell-order wave to hold unrelated follow-up structure work.
Use this slice to make `L26.2` the unambiguous next ignition target before implementation begins.

L26.2.summary: Freeze the console client-boundary, traceability, signal, and frontend-escalation contract before the next GUI follow-up implementation slice lands.
notes: Freeze the remaining GUI follow-up contract before implementation so client-boundary and traceability work do not drift.
Do not reopen shell-order simplification or frontend-stack migration questions inside this slice unless a direct conflict is proven.

L26.3.summary: Extract a shared console API client boundary and reduce repeated fetch wiring inside the current shell.
notes: Keep Reader-backed lifecycle truth canonical while reducing repeated browser fetch wiring.
Treat extraction as a maintainability boundary, not as a hidden justification for a framework migration.

L26.4.summary: Add stage-linked output trace surfaces and a shared operator-visible signal taxonomy.
notes: The trace surface should explain workflow progression rather than becoming an undifferentiated audit panel.
Shared signal badges must map to owned explanations and must not be decorative-only.

L26.5.summary: Reassess standalone frontend escalation only after the narrower follow-up lands and harden acceptance around the new client and trace contracts.
notes: Reopen standalone frontend engineering only if the narrower follow-up proves the current shell still cannot evolve safely enough.
Acceptance should verify operator comprehension and traceability, not only basic route reachability.
